title: Submodules, Nested Repositories, and Dependency Boundaries · Command Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs submodules, nested repositories, and dependency boundaries from terse repository evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the submodule dependency and trust manifest. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for submodule dependency and trust manifest after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain gitlink entries, superproject commits, submodule repositories, checked-out commits, .gitmodules, local config, and detached submodule HEAD.
Predict init, update, sync, set-url, set-branch, recursive, remote, merge, rebase, and shallow behavior across configuration layers.
Stage gitlink changes, publish dependency commits before superproject refs, review nested diffs, and verify that every referenced object is reachable.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Gita Gitlink Superproject Model · changed case
steps.instruction:
review case — Case G31 is a review in a disposable repository. the release mismatch comes from an uninitialized nested gitlink rather than branch drift. The learner must predict prefixes and object IDs expose initialized, mismatched, and conflicted nested states, run git submodule status --recursive, explain the parser submodule is uninitialized and its recorded gitlink names c0ffee0, test this changed condition: make the referenced dependency commit unreachable remotely and fail the release transfer gate, and preserve this recovery boundary: archive superproject and reachable dependency refs before URL or gitlink migration.

Which action demonstrates Gita Gitlink Superproject Model without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for explain gitlink entries, superproject commits, submodule repositories, checked-out commits, .gitmodules, local config, and detached submodule head.

content.text: Case G31 is a review in a disposable repository. the release mismatch comes from an uninitialized nested gitlink rather than branch drift. The learner must predict prefixes and object IDs expose initialized, mismatched, and conflicted nested states, run git submodule status --recursive, explain the parser submodule is uninitialized and its recorded gitlink names c0ffee0, test this changed condition: make the referenced dependency commit unreachable remotely and fail the release transfer gate, and preserve this recovery boundary: archive superproject and reachable dependency refs before URL or gitlink migration.

The learner must predict prefixes and object IDs expose initialized, mismatched, and conflicted nested states, run git submodule status --recursive, explain the parser submodule is uninitialized and its recorded gitlink names c0ffee0, test this changed condition: make the referenced dependency commit unreachable remotely and fail the release transfer gate, and preserve this recovery boundary: archive superproject and reachable dependency refs before URL or gitlink migration.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A submodule stores another repository and its full branch state inside the superproject commit.
